description: Fix Telegram double-response bug: skip callback_query updates, fix synthesizeWithToolResult to not return cleaned preamble when synthesis succeeds, and strip tool-call JSON from brainMemory commits so old tool fragments don't trigger re-execution on the next turn.
steps: In MK_OS/system/mk_entry.cpp, in the telegram_poll_loop function (around line 1200-1210 area where update types are checked), add a check to skip updates containing '"callback_query"'. This should go right after the edited_message skip logic. If the updateSlice contains '"callback_query"', set searchPos = updateBoundary and continue.

In MK_OS/orchestrator/conversation_loop.cpp, in the synthesizeWithToolResult() method, the fallback path (when synthesis response is empty) currently returns 'cleaned + \n\n + toolResult'. The problem is that 'cleaned' may contain a preamble like 'Let me check that for you' from the original LLM response. Fix: when the cleaned text looks like a preamble (short text that doesn't contain substantive content), just return toolResult alone. Specifically, if cleaned is shorter than 60 characters and doesn't contain a period or question mark, treat it as preamble and return only toolResult.

In MK_OS/orchestrator/conversation_loop.cpp, in the respond() method at Step 8 where brainMemory->commitToShortTerm('mk', response) is called, call stripToolCallJson(response) on the response BEFORE committing it. This ensures no raw tool JSON persists in conversation history. The stripToolCallJson method already exists in the class.

Add tests in MK_OS/tests/test_integration.cpp: (a) test_double_response_preamble_stripped - create an MKOrchestrator, simulate a tool call flow where synthesis fails and originalResponse contains 'Let me check that\n{"tool":...}', verify the returned text does NOT contain 'Let me check that'. (b) test_callback_query_skip - verify the pattern by checking that a string containing 'callback_query' would be detected. These can be simple string-operation tests that validate the logic without needing a full LLM.
acceptance_criteria: The main binary builds without errors using: g++ -std=c++17 -O2 -Wall -DLINUX -o mk_os system/mk_entry.cpp -lcurl -lsqlite3
All existing tests plus new tests pass with 0 failures
The telegram_poll_loop skips updates containing callback_query
synthesizeWithToolResult does not return short preamble text when synthesis fails and the preamble is under 60 chars without substantive punctuation
brainMemory commits have tool JSON stripped before storage
findings: All 3 changes implemented successfully. Made stripToolCallJson() public so tests can call it directly. 102 tests pass (99 baseline + 3 new). The pre-existing browse_url_live_fetch and web_search_news tests are network-flaky and occasionally fail due to external API availability, unrelated to this change.
